items.title: Police may use extradition to bring back overseas suspects in ‘corporate mafia’ probe, says home minister
items.summary: PENGKALAN HULU, April 12 — The police will utilise all legal instruments, including extradition, to assist in investigations into alleged corporate manipulation activities, says Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. “The investigation is proceeding well. I am briefed from time to time by the police conducting the investigation.

items.title: Hamas to meet Egyptian mediators over alleged Gaza truce violations by Israel
items.summary: GAZA CITY: A delegation from Hamas is set to hold talks with Egyptian mediators in Cairo on Sunday to address alleged Israeli violations of the Gaza ceasefire, according to two officials from the Palestinian group. This phase envisages the disarmament of Hamas and a gradual withdrawal of Israeli forces from Gaza. Meanwhile, violence persists in Gaza.

items.title: Jail time for watch gallery employee over RM2.6 million timepiece theft
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 24-year-old employee of a luxury watch gallery has been sentenced to a year's imprisonment and one stroke of the rotan after admitting to stealing 12 high-end timepieces worth RM2.6 million from his workplace in Bukit Jalil. Cheras police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said the theft took place about 12.10pm on March 31 at the watch gallery, when the suspect took advantage of his colleague's absence from the front counter. Rosdi said the suspect was arrested the following day, on April 1, by a team from the Kuala Lumpur police headquarters Criminal Investigation Department at the parking area of a mall at Bukit Damansara.

items.title: Diesel refuelling glitch in Segambut caused by empty tank, missing auto cut-off system, deputy minister says after viral claims
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 12 — A viral diesel refuelling incident at a petrol station in Jalan Segambut, Kuala Lumpur was caused by depleted fuel stock and the absence of an automatic volume detection system. She said immediate investigations by a Kuala Lumpur KPDN enforcement team found that the diesel supply had run out while refuelling was still in progress. Fuziah said checks confirmed that the actual amount of diesel dispensed was RM232.07.

items.title: Diesel hike squeezes supply chain as tofu, soya products see price increase in KL
items.summary: Kamol Cheng Thean, a vegetable and soy products trader at Pasar Harian Selayang, said prices of dry, fresh, fried and five-spiced tofu have increased by 10 sen per piece, while pressed tofu has gone up by 20 sen per piece. Tofu prices have gone up by 10 to 20 cents per piece since April 7, following sharp diesel price hikes. — Unsplash pic Soya bean drinks are also selling 50 sen higher per kilogram now, said the 55-year-old trader.

items.title: New Sarawak cancer hospital aims to serve patients across Borneo; design complete, tender next
items.summary: KUCHING, April 12 — The proposed Sarawak Cancer Centre is expected to commence construction in 2027 following the completion of its design phase, said Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib. Hanifah said the establishment of the specialised cancer hospital would strengthen ongoing cancer treatment and research efforts currently taking place at Sarawak General Hospital. “At Sarawak General Hospital here in Kuching, they already have clinical research which is actually among the first in Malaysia trying to address cancer issues.

items.title: Iran's ex-FM Zarif blames talks failure on US push to 'dictate' terms
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's former foreign minister Mohammad Javad Zarif, who led his country's delegation at 2015 nuclear talks, on Sunday blamed the failure of negotiations to end the Middle East war on US attempts to "dictate" its terms. "No negotiations - at least with Iran - will succeed based on 'our/your terms'," said Zarif, one of the architects of the nuclear deal with the United States and other world powers, which was abandoned in 2018 by US President Donald Trump. "The US must learn: you can't dictate terms to Iran.

items.title: Ketengah key driver of sustainable region development - Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Central Terengganu Development Authority (Ketengah) has strong potential to continue advancing as a key drier of sustainable regional development, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said that since its incorporation in 1973, Ketengah has played a significant role in improving the socio-economic development of rural communities in Terengganu. Zahid, who is also rural and regional development minister, said Ketengah also has strong potential to further strengthen the eco-tourism sector, supported by its natural attractions and unique local heritage.

items.title: Malaysian homeopathy facing generational gap
items.summary: LETTERS: Homeopathy in Malaysia is entering a more formal phase with the enforcement of the Traditional and Complementary Medicine Act 2016 within the framework of mandatory registration for recognised practice areas. It signals that homeopathy has been given a place within the country's traditional and complementary healthcare landscape. A major issue that is often not fully understood by the public is the generational divide within Malaysian homeopathy.

items.title: Police to focus on high-sales petrol stations near borders in new anti-smuggling move, says Saifuddin Nasution
items.summary: PENGKALAN HULU, April 12 — The Home Ministry (KDN) will assess fuel sales volumes at petrol stations near the country’s borders to implement enforcement measures aimed at curbing fuel smuggling. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the ministry will get all sales data from the Finance Ministry and the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) to identify petrol stations near the country’s borders which recorded unreasonable sales. Yesterday, Saifuddin Nasution said the KDN would deploy police personnel to petrol stations near the country’s borders to monitor and curb fuel smuggling activities.

items.title: Hassan Karim: PKR faces defining test as internal rivalries threaten party unity
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG: Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) is facing one of its most critical tests since assuming federal power, as internal rivalries and external political pressure threaten its unity and survival, Hassan Abdul Karim said today. Hassan said that differences in views and political positions among party members were inevitable, but warned that such divisions must not come at the expense of the party's stability. "The strength, unity and survival of the party must take precedence.

items.title: DBKL: Op Lens to keep Kuala Lumpur orderly, not to cut off earnings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The activities of unlicensed street photographers touting their services can cause public discomfort and pose safety risks to the community, according to Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL). "Kuala Lumpur is not just a capital city. Kuala Lumpur belongs to all of us," it said.

items.title: Iran aims to restore majority of refining capability within two months
items.summary: DUBAI: Iran expects to restore most damaged refining and distribution facilities to 70–80 per cent of their pre-attack capacity within one to two months, a senior oil official said, as authorities work to recover from a wave of strikes on energy infrastructure. Deputy Minister of Oil Mohammad Sadeq Azimifar told the Student News Network that repair work had begun and that part of the Lavan refinery is expected to resume operations within about 10 days, with other units coming back online gradually. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Saudi says east-west pipeline, energy facilities restored after attacks
items.summary: RIYADH: Saudi Arabia's energy ministry said on Sunday that its key east-west oil pipeline and other facilities had been restored following attacks by Iran on targets across the Gulf. "Energy facilities and the east-west pipeline damaged by attacks have recovered and regained their operational capacity, enhancing the reliability of supplies," the official Saudi Press Agency reported, citing an energy ministry statement. Saudi Arabia said operational and technical work had led to "restoring full pumping capacity through the east-west pipeline, amounting to approximately seven million barrels per day".

items.title: Artemis II lunar mission draws flood of conspiracy theories
items.summary: FROM false claims that a historic lunar fly-by was staged in a movie studio to unfounded narratives that footage of the crew was AI-generated, the Artemis II mission has been clouded by a blizzard of misinformation. The falsehoods – circulating across tech platforms including X, TikTok and Facebook – have also added fresh fuel to a longstanding conspiracy theory that NASA's 1969 Apollo 11 moon landing was faked. Unfounded claims that the Artemis II mission detected a mysterious moving object on the moon's surface also racked up millions of views across platforms.

items.title: Ukraine, Russia trade Easter truce breach barbs
items.summary: "As of 7am on 12 April, 2,299 ceasefire violations were recorded. Specifically: 28 enemy assault actions, 479 enemy shellings, 747 strikes by attack drones... and 1,045 strikes by FPV drones," the Ukrainian military's general staff said in a post on Facebook. "There were no missile strikes, guided aerial bomb strikes, or Shahed-type UAV strikes," it added.

items.title: Home minister: RM2m feasibility study underway for new Malaysia-Thai checkpoint at Bukit Berapit to improve safety and ease congestion
items.summary: PENGKALAN HULU, April 12 — The proposed construction of a new Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Bukit Berapit here is currently at the feasibility study stage, said Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. Also present was Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain. Meanwhile, Saifuddin Nasution said the government plans to place the operations of the Bukit Berapit ICQS under the supervision of AKPS to streamline its security control system.

items.title: Segambut petrol station in viral video: Fault due to missing auto cut-off system - Fuziah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Irregularities at a diesel pump at a petrol station on Jalan Segambut were due to the lack of an automatic cut-off feature when fuel stock was depleted. "Investigations found that the station was not equipped with an automatic volume detection system or an automatic cut-off feature when fuel stock was depleted, and instead relied on manual inspection (manual dipping). Earlier, a viral video alleging irregularities at a diesel pump at the petrol station circulated on social media.

items.title: Fadillah: We can't afford to rely on just one source of energy
items.summary: Fadillah, who is also energy transition and water transformation minister, said diversifying energy sources must be prioritised as the world increasingly faces extreme weather events that could destabilise energy supply. Fadillah said the approach was essential to ensure the country always had alternative energy sources should disruptions occur to the global energy supply structure. "Besides supplying green energy, the dam has also made significant contributions to Sarawak's economic development, particularly in the energy sector," he said.

items.title: Ramanan committed to strengthening school infrastructure, channels over RM100,000 since 2023
items.summary: The human resources minister said efforts to enhance educational facilities at the school will remain a key focus to ensure students enjoy a more conducive and quality learning environment. "School infrastructure development and support for students are important investments for the nation's future. Ramanan said RM21,800 was allocated for various school programmes, including the annual sports championship.

items.title: Padi farmers seek concrete action on diesel subsidies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Padi farmers are urging the government to grant them access to subsidised diesel, warning that rising fuel costs are placing growing pressure on the sector. The Malaysian Padi Farmers Association (Pesawah) said about 200,000 farmers nationwide are struggling with higher operating costs, particularly as they do not benefit from diesel subsidies available to other industries. "We are not like fishermen or vegetable farmers who receive fleet card facilities," said its chairman Abdul Rashid Yob.

items.title: Saudi Arabia restores east-west pipeline after Iranian attacks on energy facilities
items.summary: RIYADH: Saudi Arabia's energy ministry said on Sunday that its key east-west oil pipeline and other facilities had been restored following attacks by Iran on targets across the Gulf. "Energy facilities and the east-west pipeline damaged by attacks have recovered and regained their operational capacity, enhancing the reliability of supplies," the official Saudi Press Agency reported, citing an energy ministry statement. Saudi Arabia said operational and technical work had led to "restoring full pumping capacity through the east-west pipeline, amounting to approximately seven million barrels per day."

items.title: DBKL explains crackdown on unlicensed street photographers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 12 — The activities of unlicensed street photographers touting their services can cause public discomfort and pose safety risks to the community, according to Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL). “Kuala Lumpur is not just a capital city. Kuala Lumpur belongs to all of us,” it said.

items.title: Bukit Berapit ICQS to be upgraded after feasibility study - Saifuddin
items.summary: GERIK: The Bukit Berapit Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex here has been identified for a potential upgrade, with a RM2 million feasibility study underway to assess the suitability of the land for a new facility. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the study will determine the viability of constructing a new ICQS complex at the site, as part of broader efforts to modernise border infrastructure. Also present were Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department acting director Datuk Fisol Salleh, Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain, and Perak police chief Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin.

items.title: Woman loses RM307k in 'lucky draw' scam
items.summary: She said a man approached her, claiming to recognise her as a former fruit seller. "He asked if I recognised him and said he had previously bought fruit from me," she said. Gan said she could not recall what happened next, with events later pieced together through closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage.

items.title: Masked men rob Kelantan Welfare Department office, flee with vehicles
items.summary: Its director Che Samsuzuki Che Noh, when contacted, confirmed the incident, saying a security guard on duty at the premises was tied up, with both his hands and legs bound. Meanwhile, a source said the suspects also took the security guard's wallet containing RM300 in cash. "The robbers also took the keys to the security guard's motorcycle," the source said.

items.title: TA Securities expects profit rebound at PGF Capital
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PGF Capital Bhd's latest quarterly core profit is projected to range between RM2 million and RM4.5 million against a core loss of RM1.9 million in the same period last year, TA Securities said. TA Securites said this is expected to ease seasonally from the previous quarter's core profit of RM7.8 million, as PGF Capital's shipments to Australia typically slow during the Christmas and New Year holiday period. The firm said that PGF Capital's shipments were unaffected in the first quarter of 2026.

items.title: War in the Middle East: US-Iran negotiations fail as conflict intensifies across regions
items.summary: - Teheran DOWNPLAYS NO-DEAL FINISH - Iran's foreign ministry said no one had held any expectation that talks with the United States could have reached an agreement within one session after the negotiations in Islamabad stalled. - PAKISTAN URGES US, IRAN - Pakistan's foreign minister insisted that Washington and Teheran must uphold a ceasefire agreement, after marathon talks between the two sides to end the war in the Middle East ended without a deal. - IRAN SAYS TALKS HINGED ON AVOIDING 'EXCESSIVE DEMANDS' - The success of peace talks between the United States and Iran depended on Washington avoiding "excessive" and "unlawful" demands, the Iranian foreign ministry's spokesman said early on Sunday after marathon talks in Islamabad.

items.title: Corporate mafia probe: Two-pronged investigation under Amla and criminal laws
items.summary: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the investigation is ongoing, with police pursuing all leads, including recording statements from those believed to be involved, both in the country and abroad. "First, is the police investigating this matter? The police are conducting the investigation in a two-pronged approach.

items.title: Diabetes-linked kidney diseases on the rise, cost Malaysia RM3.3b a year, says health minister
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 12 — The cost of treating end-stage chronic kidney disease (CKD) in Malaysia has surged sharply from RM572 million in 2020 to RM3.3 billion annually at present, following a steady rise in cases each year. “In 2010, Malaysia spent RM572 million on treating end-stage CKD, but today the figure has risen to RM3.3 billion annually. “This burden not only affects patients’ quality of life, but also places a very heavy financial strain on our country,” he said when officiating the National World Kidney Day 2026 observance at Wisma Majlis Bandaraya Shah Alam (MBSA) here today.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of short-term severe thunderstorms nationwide until evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 12 — Thunderstorms, coupled with heavy rains and strong winds are expected nationwide until 5pm today, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) warned in a weather alert this afternoon. Affected areas include Kedah (Baling, Kulim), Perak (Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim), and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Bentong, Bera and Rompin). Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Gombak and Hulu Langat), Kuala Lumpur, Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Kuala Pilah and Jempol) and Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Mersing and Kota Tinggi) are also expected to be affected.

items.title: Hungarians vote in closely watched election with Orban's rule on line
items.summary: BUDAPEST: Hungarians began voting on Sunday in closely watched parliamentary elections that could end nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban's 16-year stint in power as a self-described "thorn" in the EU's side. Opinion polls suggest the Tisza party of pro-European conservative Peter Magyar, who has promised a "system change", is running well ahead of Orban's. US Vice President JD Vance visited Budapest earlier this week to rally with Orban.

items.title: Artemis crew urges unity on Earth after historic lunar flyby
items.summary: HOUSTON: Artemis II astronauts expressed awe on Saturday over their record-setting lunar flyby mission, urging unity on Earth after witnessing the planet's isolation like a "lifeboat" in space. The crew spoke at a news conference as NASA took a victory lap following the success of the mission. Flanked on stage by mission commander Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover and Jeremy Hansen, astronaut Christina Koch called for people on Earth to embrace their shared humanity.

items.title: Ex-Kelantan footballer, friend claim trial to abducting, confining 45-year-old man in Tumpat
items.summary: The prosecution was conducted by Deputy Public Prosecutor Siti Aiysha Na’ilah Harizan. Cik Hisammudin was represented by lawyer Heather Marie Anthony, while Saufi was unrepresented. Earlier, Siti Aiysha Na’ilah proposed bail at RM20,000 for each accused, citing the seriousness of the case.

items.title: Ex-Kelantan footballer charged with kidnapping gold shop owner
items.summary: PASIR MAS: A former Kelantan footballer and his accomplice were charged at the Sessions Court today with kidnapping a gold shop owner in May last year. Che Hisammudin Hassan, 51, a former Kelantan football striker, and Saufi Ariffin, 24, both pleaded not guilty after the charge was read before Judge Zulkpli Abdullah. They were accused of forcibly abducting Marzuki Mustapha with intent to cause him to be secretly and wrongfully confined at 7.20am along a roadside in Kampung Palekbang, Tumpat, on May 20 last year.

items.title: Saifuddin: Border petrol stations with unusual sales under police watch
items.summary: GERIK: Petrol stations along Malaysia's borders recording unusually high fuel sales volumes will be placed under closer police scrutiny as part of a nationwide crackdown on smuggling, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. He said enforcement agencies would prioritise stations with abnormal petrol and diesel sales using data shared by the Finance Ministry (MOF) and the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN). Saifuddin said KPDN regulates about 4,000 petrol stations nationwide, while MOF oversees fuel subsidies, with both agencies holding relevant data.

items.title: Malaysia faces RM3.3bil kidney disease burden each year
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Malaysia is now spending RM3.3 billion annually on end-stage kidney disease treatment, a sharp rise from RM572 million in 2010, highlighting the growing burden of chronic illness on the country's healthcare system. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said chronic kidney disease (CKD) has become one of the most pressing health challenges facing Malaysians today. "Every day, 28 Malaysians are diagnosed with kidney failure and must begin dialysis treatment," he said at the national-level World Kidney Day 2026 event in Shah Alam.

items.title: Cambodia urges Thailand to resume talks on disputed border
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: Cambodia has urged Thailand to resume talks as soon as possible on their disputed border, a long-standing disagreement that led to deadly clashes last year. Cambodia is fully ready," he said in a statement posted on social media. Thailand's Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow said earlier Saturday that his country was not ready to resume talks, even though a new government had just been formed.

items.title: A Dungun farmer counts RM20,000 in losses as hot weather wipes out his crops
items.summary: DUNGUN, April 12 — A farmer has suffered an estimated RM20,000 in losses after his vegetable crops cultivated in Kampung Minda near here were damaged due to the current hot and dry weather affecting the country. He said most of the crops were damaged as water sources from nearby swamps and rivers had nearly dried up due to the hot weather, affecting the irrigation system on his farm. In addition, he said the hot weather has also made crops more susceptible to pest attacks, further affecting growth and yield.

items.title: Khaled questions if Malaysians ready for 'uncertain future' following peace talk breakdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin has called for a serious reflection on national resilience following the reported failure of negotiations between Iran and the United States in Islamabad. In a statement on his official Facebook page, the defence minister noted that the breakdown in talks between the two nations signals a period of heightened global instability. Khaled raised questions regarding the preparedness of Malaysians to navigate the ripples of such geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Negotiation snag? US demanded Iran drop all nuclear programmes, including medical use
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The United States had demanded during peace negotiations with Iran in Islamabad that Teheran drop its nuclear programme entirely. Al Jazeera reported that the proposed 10-point framework Iran had come up with stopped short of a complete abandonment of its nuclear ambitions. However, the US demands included that Iran all nuclear programmes, civilian or otherwise.

items.title: Remand extended for former CEO, chairman in MACC probe into RM300m share transaction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 12 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has obtained a court order to extend the remand of a former chief executive officer (CEO) of a government-linked statutory body and a company chairman over suspected involvement in a share sale transaction. The MACC said the two suspects are believed to have conspired with several board members and shareholders of a company in the sale of shares to the statutory body. According to the commission, a five-day remand order until April 16, 2026, was issued by Magistrate Ainul Izzati Mohd Jamili at the Putrajaya Magistrates’ Court this morning, following an application by MACC after the initial remand period ended today.

items.title: Police believe escaped Pakistani detainee still in Malaysia, 62 hours after Sungai Buloh breakout
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 12 — A Pakistani remand detainee who escaped from Sungai Buloh Prison on Thursday is believed to still be in the country. Sungai Buloh Police chief Supt Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said a nationwide manhunt is under way to track down the 24-year-old suspect, Muhammad Hassan. “The search area is still within the country,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Smuggling crackdown nets RM4.6m in goods, 617 arrests; Sabah, Labuan record highest cases
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 12 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) arrested 617 individuals and seized goods valued at RM4.6 million during Ops Khas Pagar Laut 1/2026 conducted from Feb 27 to April 10. Mohd Rosli said the highest number of cases involved offences under the Fisheries Act 1985 at 68, followed by 39 cases under the Merchant Shipping Ordinance 1952 and 23 under the Immigration Act 1959/63. Mohd Rosli said Sabah and Labuan MMEA recorded the highest number of cases at 37, followed by Johor with 33, and Kedah and Perlis with 24 each.

items.title: Brace for stormy week as monsoon transition begins, warns disaster management chief
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said based on forecasts by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), weather conditions are likely to include thunderstorms and heavy rain in most areas, particularly in the western and inland parts of peninsular Malaysia, including Johor and Melaka, as well as inland Sabah and Sarawak. He said the weather changes would help ease the recent hot conditions, although some northern and inland areas of the peninsula may still experience higher temperatures. “At the same time, we must remain vigilant as these conditions could bring heavy rain, strong winds and thunderstorms within a short period,” he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: Car crashes into tree in Sibu, driver killed on the spot
items.summary: SIBU, April 12 — A woman was killed after the car she was driving crashed into a tree at Lorong Lanang 61, Jalan Lanang here at midnight last night. Sibu Zone Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) chief Andy Alie said the victim, aged 46, died on the spot in the accident and her body was pinned to the driver’s seat. He said the department received an emergency call at 12.45 am before firefighters from the Sibu Sentral station were deployed to the scene.

items.title: Johor must act now on pay and policies or face talent drain to Singapore when RTS starts, state economic adviser warns
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 12 — Johor must refine its cross-border economic strategy and sharpen policy clarity to address the outflow of skilled labour ahead of the planned launch of the Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link early next year. Johor Economic, Tourism and Cultural Office (Jetco) Singapore Executive chairman Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad said the RTS Link, which will connect Johor Bahru and Singapore, is set to boost cross-border mobility and could accelerate the migration of skilled workers from Johor to the city-state. Currently, about 400,000 Malaysians commute daily across the Johor Causeway to work in Singapore.

items.title: Diesel aid review underway to widen eligibility under BUDI95 as subsidy cost hits RM6b, says Finance Ministry official
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, April 12 — The government is conducting a comprehensive study of proposals to expand the diesel subsidy scheme to ensure that more affected groups, including farmers, benefit from the initiative. The Finance Minister’s political secretary, Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim, said the government continues to listen to grievances from groups affected by rising diesel prices and is identifying ways to assist them. Meanwhile, Muhammad Kamil said the government is also taking into account current fiscal challenges, as expenditure to cushion rising fuel prices has surged from about RM700 million to RM6 billion.

items.title: Varsity student under seven-day watch over Sepang stabbing as police record statements from 10 witnesses; victim in ICU
items.summary: SEPANG, April 12 — Police have detained a private university student suspected of stabbing a woman at an apartment in Bandar Sunsuria here on Friday. Sepang district police chief ACP Norhizam Bahaman said in a statement today that the 22-year-old man was arrested on the day of the incident. He said the Sepang District Police Headquarters (IPD) confirmed receiving a report regarding a 34-year-old woman who was injured with a sharp object at her residence on Friday.

items.title: Sultan of Pahang presents donation to family of late soldier Muhammad Amirul Raziq
items.summary: KUANTAN, April 12 — The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, has presented a donation to the family of the late Muhammad Amirul Raziq Rosafindi, who passed away at Sultan Haji Ahmad Shah Hospital (HoSHAS), Temerloh, yesterday. The Pahang Sultanate, in a Facebook post, said the donation was presented by Palace Religious officer Lt Col Anuar Mohd Ehwal to the late Muhammad Amirul Raziq’s father, Rosafindi Tambi Chik, at Felda Sungai Kelamah Mosque, Gemas, at about 3 am today. The post said Private Muhammad Amirul Raziq, who was attached to the 25th Battalion, Royal Malay Regiment (RAMD) in Bentong, died at 9.34 am yesterday after nearly two weeks of battling for his life following serious injuries.

items.title: Police detain three after woman attacked during live stream in Sungai Petani
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, April 12 — Police have detained three individuals to assist in the investigation into a case involving a woman who was attacked while conducting a live broadcast on social media from inside her car in Bandar Laguna Merbok near here yesterday afternoon. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the victim was allegedly attacked by suspects travelling in a Toyota Vellfire at 5.02pm in front of a house in the area. The incident has gone viral on social media since yesterday, showing a video recording of a woman conducting a live broadcast from inside a car being attacked and assaulted by another woman.

items.title: Johor–Singapore economic zone takes shape: What it could mean for jobs, wages and growth
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 12 — Strong multi-stakeholder collaboration, anchored on a holistic ecosystem approach, is set to accelerate Johor’s economic transformation, particularly through the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ). Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) Singapore director T. Vinothan said aligning government, industry players and investors is critical to ensuring the seamless implementation of initiatives across investment, talent and digital transformation. Also serving as panellists were Johor Corporation chief talent officer Najmie Noordin and Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC) Regional Digital Economy Office director Raja Segaran.

items.title: As costs bite, DBKL halves rent for 10,000 traders till Dec 31, 2027
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 12 — Some 10,000 traders under the supervision of Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) will receive a 50 per cent rental reduction for two years, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh. She said DBKL and Labuan Corporation are among the earliest local authorities (PBTs) to announce the initiative, aimed at easing the cost-of-living burden on traders, particularly following rising fuel prices. On today’s programme, Hannah said the government has also launched free sports classes involving sepak takraw and skipping in Putrajaya as a targeted effort to address obesity among children.

items.title: Rising fuel costs threatening seafood supply, Sarawak trawler operators appeal for urgent aid
items.summary: KUCHING, April 12 — Sarawak’s deep-sea fishing industry is on the brink of collapse as soaring diesel prices have forced most Category C2 vessels to halt operations, prompting urgent calls for targeted fuel subsidies to avert a seafood supply crunch. According to Sarawak Fishing Vessels Association chairman Harry Tan, fewer than 30 C2 (deep-sea fishing) vessels remain licensed statewide, with many already ceasing operations as industrial diesel approaches RM7 per litre. He said the situation had become critical for deep-sea operators, who are required to fish beyond 30 nautical miles from shore, resulting in significantly higher fuel consumption and costs.

items.title: Damaged passports, missing documents: Malaysia’s border control turns away Thai, Bangladeshi travellers at Bukit Kayu Hitam
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 12 — The Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) denied entry to 28 foreign nationals at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex between April 1 and yesterday (April 11). Bukit Kayu Hitam AKPS commander SAC Fauzi Mohd Yusof said that of the total, 14 individuals were Indian nationals, comprising 13 men and one woman. According to him, another five Thai nationals were denied entry after being found to be using damaged passports and failing to meet the country’s entry requirements.

items.title: Woman suffers broken nose after attack outside Mersing supermarket, cops nab suspect
items.summary: MERSING, April 12 — Police have arrested a local man for allegedly punching a woman, resulting in serious injuries in an incident outside a supermarket here last Friday. Mersing deputy district police chief DSP Mohd Nizam Yusoff said acting on a police report lodged by the victim, the 47-year-old man was arrested in the town at about 12.15 am on Saturday. Mohd Nizam said the 48-year-old victim sustained bruises on his forehead and a broken nose following the incident.

items.title: Education Ministry to relocate SK Balaban Jaya students after safety concerns over ground movement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 12 — The Education Ministry (KPM) will temporarily relocate pupils of SK Balaban Jaya in Beluran, Sabah, following safety concerns linked to infrastructure damage caused by soil and slope movement. In a statement yesterday, the ministry said it is concerned over issues involving access to education and safety at the school, adding that the damage has rendered parts of the school unsafe for use. As an immediate measure, KPM said pupils will be temporarily transferred to SK Pekan Beluran, with transportation provided to ensure students can attend school without disruption or burdening parents.

items.title: Six Pakistani nationals arrested over viral fight in Beluran shop incident
items.summary: BELURAN, April 12 — Police have arrested six Pakistani nationals suspected of being involved in a fight that went viral on social media in front of a grocery shop in Kampung Kubambang, Paitan, on Friday, according to Sinar Harian. Beluran district police chief Superintendent Hasan Majid said police received a report on the incident at 6.49 pm the same day. Hasan said following the incident and a viral video, police managed to detain all six individuals identified as being involved in the scuffle.

items.title: UiTM Kelantan reassures safety after shooting incident outside Machang campus
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 12 — Security controls remain in place at Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) Kelantan’s Machang campus following a recent shooting involving police and a crime suspect outside the institution, Sinar Harian reported. UiTM deputy vice-chancellor for industry, community and alumni network, Prof Ir Ts Dr Juliana Johari, said the incident occurred outside the campus perimeter and did not involve students or any internal university areas. She said security on campus is continuously enforced, including regular patrols by auxiliary police and monitoring by management and the student representative council.

items.title: Man with mental health issues critically injured after falling from fifth-floor flat in Jelutong
items.summary: In the 6.53 pm incident, the victim is said to have fallen and landed on the roof of the building’s first floor. According to the Malay daily, Penang Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director John Sagun Francis said the department received an emergency call at 6.53 pm before a team from the Jalan Perak fire station was dispatched to the scene. “The fire engine arrived about 10 minutes later and found the victim on the first-floor roof after he is believed to have fallen from the fifth floor.
